Specific human voice vs committee 4/10 — Reads like marketing copy with TL;DR, Key takeaways, FAQ scaffolding; occasional first-person flashes but mostly institutional.

suggestedPatch.target: A real-world, day-by-day 7-day Oahu itinerary for friend groups: grounded in actual places, targeted for group organisers, and structured for minimal chaos.
suggestedPatch.before: A real-world, day-by-day 7-day Oahu itinerary for friend groups: grounded in actual places, targeted for group organisers, and structured for minimal chaos.
suggestedPatch.after: I've planned this trip twice for friends—here's the day-by-day that actually survived contact with a group chat.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Specific human voice vs committee" scored 4/10: Reads like marketing copy with TL;DR, Key takeaways, FAQ scaffolding; occasional first-person flashes but mostly institutional.

evidence: Genuine opinions vs safe claims 5/10 — Some real takes (rentals beat hotels, Rainbow Drive-In) but mostly hedged, evenhanded advice.

suggestedPatch.target: Oahu is group- and family-friendly: beaches, parks, and safe neighborhoods.
suggestedPatch.before: Oahu is group- and family-friendly: beaches, parks, and safe neighborhoods.
suggestedPatch.after: Oahu works for both, but families ruin Hanauma Bay's 7am slot—book the 10am if you're a group that drinks the night before.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Genuine opinions vs safe claims" scored 5/10: Some real takes (rentals beat hotels, Rainbow Drive-In) but mostly hedged, evenhanded advice.

evidence: Unexpected phrasings 5/10 — "Herding cats," "hangry mutinies," "sane, sunburned" show some life, but most sentences are travel-blog boilerplate.
Point of view vs survey 5/10 — Picks a lane on rentals vs hotels, but most sections survey options neutrally.

suggestedPatch.target: Waikiki: easy for first-timers, steps to food and main beaches. Ala Moana: better value, easier for relaxed group hangouts. Kailua: only practical if you get a rental; ideal for groups set on kayaking/beach mornings.
suggestedPatch.before: Waikiki: easy for first-timers, steps to food and main beaches. Ala Moana: better value, easier for relaxed group hangouts. Kailua: only practical if you get a rental; ideal for groups set on kayaking/beach mornings.
suggestedPatch.after: Skip Waikiki unless someone in the group has never seen Hawaii—Ala Moana rentals are cheaper, quieter, and you still walk to everything.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Point of view vs survey" scored 5/10: Picks a lane on rentals vs hotels, but most sections survey options neutrally.

evidence: Quotable sentence 4/10 — The "screenshot of Duke's Waikiki" line is genuinely quotable; most others are functional.

suggestedPatch.target: Plan Oahu by the day, not the vibe, actual places get you out of group chat limbo.
suggestedPatch.before: Plan Oahu by the day, not the vibe, actual places get you out of group chat limbo.
suggestedPatch.after: Plan Oahu by the day, not the vibe—otherwise your only concrete plan becomes a screenshot of Duke's Waikiki.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Quotable sentence" scored 4/10: The "screenshot of Duke's Waikiki" line is genuinely quotable; most others are functional.

evidence: Intro earns attention 3/10 — Opens with meta-chrome (TL;DR, byline, date, read-time) before any hook; actual hook is buried paragraphs down.

suggestedPatch.target: TL;DR: A solid 7-day Oahu group trip itinerary balances iconic beaches like Waikiki and Lanikai with less-crowded spots like Makapuu and Kailua to keep everyone happy without endless group chat debates.
suggestedPatch.before: TL;DR: A solid 7-day Oahu group trip itinerary balances iconic beaches like Waikiki and Lanikai with less-crowded spots like Makapuu and Kailua to keep everyone happy without endless group chat debates.
suggestedPatch.after: By day three, your group chat is 400 messages deep and the only confirmed plan is a screenshot of Duke's. Here's the fix.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Intro earns attention" scored 3/10: Opens with meta-chrome (TL;DR, byline, date, read-time) before any hook; actual hook is buried paragraphs down.
